clean_story:
Time’s shadow. What follows the now.

The word after comes from Old English æfter, meaning "behind in place or time"—like footsteps trailing a walker, always tied to what came before.

After isn’t just sequence; it’s the hinge between cause and effect. A struck match after oxygen meets flame. A plant bending after sunlight shifts direction. Even silence has an after: the hum in your ears after loud music stops, proof that absence is still part of the pattern.

Mechanisms rely on it. A camera’s shutter closes after light exposes the film. Your brain fires neurons after sensing heat—not during. The delay is measurable: touch a hot stove, and pain registers 0.15 seconds after contact. That gap is where decisions hide.

Three related subjects are cause and effect, memory as echo, and how delays shape reactions.
